title: Incorrect Node Deletion in Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given the root node of a binary tree, write a function that deletes a target node and returns the root of the resulting tree. If the target node does not exist, return the original tree.
statement.inputFormat: Input format: tree nodes with values and left/right children
statement.outputFormat: Output format: root of the modified tree
statement.notes: Delete the target node and its children, and fix tree properties after deletion

examples.explanation: Delete the node with value 5 and its children

hints.text: First hint text: check if the target node exists
Second hint text: use DFS to find the target node

editorial.title: Editorial title
commonMistakes: Not fixing tree properties after deletion
